name: Get Option Greek
summary: Calculates option Greeks (call/put price, delta, gamma, theta, rho, vega) for a given option contract via a POST call. Inputs include expiry date, strike price, spot price, interest rate, volatility and option type; the response returns both call and put values for each Greek.

request_params.name: jData*
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list

response_fields.name: request_time
response_fields.description: This will be present only in a successful response.

notes: Section spans page 55 (title GET OPTION GREEK through the success response) into page 56 (RESPONSE DETAILS table + sample failure response), ending where EXCH MSG begins on page 56. The 'Possible value' column is empty for every jData field and every response field in the source table (including stat), so all possible_values are left blank to stay faithful to the doc. From the sample responses, stat takes 'OK' (success) or 'Not_Ok' (failure). Field descriptions are the terse one-word labels exactly as printed in the PDF (int_rate = 'Init Rate', cal_vego/put_vego = 'Cal Vego'/'Put Vego'). The sample failure response is the generic 'jData is Missing' error rendered in the page-56 right column, not a Greek-specific failure.
issues: The 'Possible value' column is empty for every jData field and every response field (including stat) in the source table, so no enums could be captured (none exist in the doc). stat's OK / Not_Ok values appear only in the sample responses, not the table, so possible_values is left blank. Field descriptions are terse one-word labels as given in the PDF. The sample_failure_response is the generic 'jData is Missing' error rendered in the page-56 right column, not a Greek-specific failure.
